DISCOVERY 302, DEC-31-2024 RE: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE OF ZUCKERBERG SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL AND TRAUMA CENTER WITH THE COURTS NOVEMBER 1, 2024 ORDER TO COMPLY WITH THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR THE PRODUCTION OF RECORDS. THE SUBJECT MATTER IS DISCUSSED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT THE RELIEF CITED IN THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING DOES NOT APPLY TO THE SUBJECT MOTION AND OFFERS CLARIFICATION AND ADVOCACY THEREON. THE COURT CONFIRMS THAT THERE IS NO OPPOSITION TO THE SUBJECT MOTION REFERENCED BY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. THE COURT WITHDRAWS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND GRANTS THE RELIEF SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS INSTRUCTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT VIA EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/CEK)

Off Calendar May-29-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-26-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Compliance Of Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital And Trauma Center With The Courts November 1, 2024 Order To Comply With The Deposition Subpoena For The Production Of Records
Off Calendar Dec-31-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT DAVID GUARDINO'S Motion To Compel Compliance Of Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital And Trauma Center With The Courts November 1, 2024 Order To Comply With The Deposition Subpoena For The Production Of Records.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Khachatur Ayvazyan, Esq., of Megeredchian Law, APC., (818) 530-1300, for Plaintiff.

Sara K. Batres, Esq., of Bretoi, Lutz & Stele, (866) 543-0394, for Defendant.

The subject matter is discussed. Counsel for Defendant claims that the relief cited in the court's tentative ruling does not apply to the subject motion and offers clarification and advocacy thereon. The court confirms that there is no opposition to the subject motion referenced by Defendant's counsel.

The court withdraws its tentative ruling and grants the relief sought by Defendant. Counsel for Defendant is instructed to submit a proposed order to the court via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/CEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Sara K. Batres, Esq. In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Dec-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re:DEFENDANT DAVID GUARDINO's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made. The court adopts the tentative ruling.

David Guardino's motion to compel compliance with deposition subpoena and for sanctions is granted; no timely opposition filed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge:Richard B Ulmer Jr; Clerk: K.Valdes; Not Reported.(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Sara K. Batres, Esq. In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-01-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. KHACHATUR CHRIS AYVAZYAN, Esq. of Megeredchain Law, APC. (tel: 818-530-1300) for Plaintiff.
Atty. SARA BATRES, Esq. of Bretol, Lutz & Stele (tel: 866-543-0394) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT DAVID GUARDINO's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Matter not contested/argued. Both counsel stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem and authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf. The Court states it has received from counsel Sara Batres the requested proof of service. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is granted and sanctions are awarded (see signed order for complete ruling).

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Jeffrey Wohl; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
